notes: scan_film with the HANATOS2025 LOG-EXPOSURE-CORRECTION SURFACE ON (settings.apply_hanatos2025_adaptation_surface=True; window left ON). Exercises the apply_surface branch of compute_hanatos2025_tc_lut: after the window-weighted spectra contraction, the filming tc_lut is multiplied per-cell, per-channel by 2**surface, where surface = eval_poly4_log_exposure_surface(profile.hanatos2025_adaptation_surface_params, illuminant_xy=_illuminant_to_xy(film.reference_illuminant), model='poly4') — a degree-4 2D polynomial over the (L,L) tc grid, centred at tri2quad(illuminant_xy), passed through hanika_sigmoid(raw, max=2.0). This changes the filming tc_lut and therefore film_log_raw / film_density_cmy / final_rgb. The surface is independent of deactivate_spatial_effects (it is in the LUT-build path), so spatial + grain are kept off and the case stays deterministic/bit-stable. Tested by tests/test_hanatos_surface_e2e.cpp.
